title: Data Table
description: Foldkit-native shadcn Data Table composition helpers and examples built on the local Table primitive plus a small query-state model for filtering, sorting, selection, visibility, and pagination.
markdown:
# Data Table

## Overview

Data Table is a Foldkit-native translation of the shadcn guide, not a giant
one-size grid component. It pairs the local `shadcn/table` primitive with a
small query-state helper for filtering, sorting, pagination, column visibility,
and row selection.

## Foldkit Model

`DataTableState` is Schema-backed and owns only table-query concerns:
sorting, string filters, hidden columns, selected row ids, and pagination.
Row data stays in the parent model or in example fixtures. The helper functions
stay pure so a parent update function can handle every interaction as a fact.

## Usage

Import the query helpers and compose them with the local table parts.

```ts
const state = DataTable.initialState({ pageSize: 10 })
const model = DataTable.rowModel({
  rows,
  columns,
  state,
  rowId: row => row.id,
})
```

Render `model.visibleColumns` with `shadcn/table`, drive filter inputs with
`setFilter`, sorting buttons with `toggleSort`, row checkboxes with
`toggleRowSelection`, and pagination controls with `previousPage`, `nextPage`,
`firstPage`, `lastPage`, and `setPageSize`.

## Examples

The registry includes:

- `DataTableDemo` for payments with email filtering, selection, sorting, and pagination
- `DataTableTasks` for a denser task workflow with badges, column visibility, and rows-per-page controls
- `DataTableRtl` for Arabic copy and RTL table layout

## Accessibility

Examples keep native table semantics by composing real `table`, `thead`,
`tbody`, `tr`, `th`, and `td` elements from the local Table primitive. Row
selection uses accessible checkbox controls, and filter/page controls keep
visible text labels or aria labels for screen readers.

## Foldkit Differences

The origin guide uses TanStack React Table, React state, Lucide React icons,
and follow-up dashboard integrations. This registry item replaces the row-model
logic with local pure helpers, uses Foldkit messages as facts, and keeps
installable source free of React, TanStack, chart, drawer, toast, and drag
dependencies.

## Follow-up: Native DragAndDrop and VirtualList

Future reorder examples should use native `@foldkit/ui/DragAndDrop` and commit
row moves back through parent update logic.

Future large-row examples should use native `@foldkit/ui/VirtualList` inside a
constrained-height scrolling container.

Neither reorder nor virtualization is part of first-slice acceptance for this
Data Table item.

registry.reason: Examples render payment, task status, and priority surfaces with local Badge helpers.
Examples use local Button helpers for sorting, actions, and pagination controls.
The tasks example presents the denser table workflow inside a local Card layout.
Examples use local Checkbox helpers for row selection and page selection.
Examples use the local Dropdown Menu primitive for column visibility controls.
Examples use local Input helpers for column filter controls.
Examples use local Pagination helpers for first, previous, next, and last page controls.
Examples use the local Select primitive for rows-per-page choices.
Data Table composes the local Table primitive for its rendering surface.
The query model exports local layout class helpers through the shared cn utility.

examples.title: DataTableDemo
examples.description: Payments table with email filtering, sortable columns, selection, and pagination.
examples.snippet:
export const DataTableDemo = <Message = never>(
  controller: DataTableExampleController<Message> = {},
): Html =>
  renderDataTable({
    controller,
    state: stateFor(controller.state, defaultPaymentState),
    rows: paymentRows,
    columns: paymentColumns,
    rowId: paymentRowId,
    copy: paymentCopy,
    filterColumnId: 'email',
    actionLabelForRow: () => 'Copy ID',
    actionIdForRow: payment => `copy-payment:${payment.id}`,
  })

examples.title: DataTableTasks
examples.description: Task table with badges, column visibility, and rows-per-page controls.
examples.snippet:
export const DataTableTasks = <Message = never>(
  controller: DataTableExampleController<Message> = {},
): Html =>
  renderDataTable({
    controller,
    state: stateFor(controller.state, defaultTaskState),
    rows: taskRows,
    columns: taskColumns,
    rowId: taskRowId,
    copy: taskCopy,
    filterColumnId: 'title',
    title: 'Tasks',
    description:
      'A denser task table with badges, title filtering, column toggles, and page-size controls.',
    card: true,
    actionLabelForRow: () => 'Open',
    actionIdForRow: task => `open-task:${task.id}`,
  })

examples.title: DataTableRtl
examples.description: RTL payments table with Arabic copy, selection, filtering, and pagination.
examples.snippet:
export const DataTableRtl = <Message = never>(
  controller: DataTableExampleController<Message> = {},
): Html =>
  renderDataTable({
    controller,
    state: stateFor(controller.state, defaultRtlState),
    rows: paymentRows,
    columns: paymentColumnsRtl,
    rowId: paymentRowId,
    copy: paymentRtlCopy,
    filterColumnId: 'email',
    actionLabelForRow: () => 'نسخ المعرّف',
    actionIdForRow: payment => `copy-payment-rtl:${payment.id}`,
  })

deviations.summary: Data Table is accepted from docs and example evidence because no primary base-nova component source file exists.
deviations.rationale: The origin docs position Data Table as a composition guide rather than a single reusable primitive.

deviations.summary: Installable source replaces TanStack React Table row models and React state with a local Foldkit query-state helper.
deviations.rationale: Filtering, sorting, selection, visibility, and pagination must stay installable without React or TanStack runtime dependencies.

deviations.summary: Dashboard row reordering, chart, drawer, and toast integrations are deferred from first-slice acceptance.
deviations.rationale: Those flows need separate native foundations and follow-up examples beyond the base Data Table guide.

deviations.summary: Installable source uses local inline SVG icons instead of Lucide React or Tabler React icons.
deviations.rationale: The examples preserve the visible affordances while keeping the registry item installable.
